descriptions.groups_create: Create a new group under your Zoom account. Required: name (in the request body). Returns the new group's id and name. Groups organize users for shared settings and policies; create the group first, then add members with groups_members_*. Requires account-level (admin) auth.
descriptions.groups_groups: List all groups under your Zoom account, with each group's id, name, and total_members count. Read-only. Use this to discover group ids before calling groups_members_* or groups_get. Requires account-level (admin) auth.
descriptions.h323_device-list: List the H.323/SIP room-system devices registered on your Zoom account, returning each device's id, name, protocol (H.323 or SIP), and encryption setting. Read-only. Use to audit conference-room hardware before updating or deleting a device.
descriptions.im_group-create: Create a new IM (instant messaging) directory group under your account. Required: name (in the request body). Returns the new IM group's id. IM groups control chat visibility between users; distinct from meeting/user groups. Requires account-level (admin) auth.
descriptions.im_groups: List the IM (instant messaging) directory groups under your account, returning each group's id, name, type, and total_members. Read-only. Distinct from meeting groups (groups_groups). Requires account-level (admin) auth.
descriptions.meetings_invitation_meeting: Retrieve the formatted text invitation (join URL, dial-in numbers, meeting ID, password) for a scheduled meeting. Required: meetingId (path). Read-only. Returns an object with a single 'invitation' string suitable for pasting into a calendar invite or email.
descriptions.meetings_meeting: Retrieve full details for a single meeting: topic, type, status, start_time, duration, timezone, join_url, settings, and host id. Required: meetingId (path). Optional: occurrence_id for a specific instance of a recurring meeting. Read-only. Use this when you already have a meeting id and need its current configuration.
descriptions.meetings_polls_meeting: List all polls configured on a meeting, returning each poll's id, title, status, and questions. Required: meetingId (path). Read-only. Use this to discover poll ids before meetings_polls_meeting-get or meetings_polls_meeting-update.
descriptions.meetings_polls_meeting-get: Retrieve a single poll on a meeting, including its title, status, and full question/answer-option list. Required: meetingId and pollId (both path). Read-only. Use meetings_polls_meeting first if you don't already have the pollId.
descriptions.meetings_polls_meeting-update: Replace a meeting's poll definition (title and the full questions array) in place. Required: meetingId and pollId (path); title and questions (body). Returns HTTP 204 (no body) on success. This overwrites the poll, so send the complete questions list, not a partial patch.
descriptions.meetings_recordings_get: Retrieve all cloud recordings for a meeting: each recording file's id, type (MP4/M4A/TIMELINE/CHAT/TRANSCRIPT), download_url, play_url, file_size, and recording_start/end times. Required: meetingId (path; accepts a meeting id or UUID). Read-only. Requires the recording:read scope and cloud recording enabled.
descriptions.meetings_recordings_status-update: Recover a meeting's cloud recordings that were previously moved to trash. Required: meetingId (path); action='recover' (body). Returns HTTP 204 (no body) on success. This is a state change, not a read — use meetings_recordings_get to inspect recordings first.
descriptions.meetings_status_meeting: Change a live or scheduled meeting's status. Required: meetingId (path); action (body) — 'end' to end an in-progress meeting or 'recover' to restore a deleted one. Returns HTTP 204 (no body) on success. Use this to end a meeting programmatically rather than waiting for the host.
descriptions.past-meetings_details: Retrieve summary details of a meeting that has already ended: uuid, id, host_id, topic, type, start_time, end_time, duration, and total participant_count. Required: meetingUUID (path; double-encode UUIDs containing '/' or '//'). Read-only. Use past-meetings_instances_past-meetings first to find the UUID of a specific past occurrence.
descriptions.past-meetings_instances_past-meetings: List every ended instance (occurrence) of a recurring meeting, returning each instance's uuid and start_time. Required: meetingId (path). Read-only. Each returned uuid feeds past-meetings_details, past-meetings_participants_*, or report_meeting-details.
descriptions.past-webinars_instances_past-webinars: List every ended instance (occurrence) of a recurring webinar, returning each instance's uuid and start_time. Required: webinarId (path). Read-only. Each returned uuid feeds the past-webinars detail and participant endpoints or report_webinar-details.
descriptions.report_meeting-details: Retrieve the post-meeting usage report for an ended meeting: host, topic, scheduled vs. actual start/end, duration, total participants, and unique-user count. Required: meetingId (path; accepts id or UUID). Read-only. Reporting endpoints require a paid plan and the report:read:admin scope; data is the billable-usage view, distinct from meetings_meeting's configuration view.
descriptions.report_meeting-polls: Retrieve every participant's poll responses for an ended meeting, grouped by participant with their name, email, and per-question answers. Required: meetingId (path). Read-only. Requires a paid plan and report:read:admin scope. Use meetings_polls_meeting for the poll definitions; this returns the collected answers.
descriptions.report_webinar-details: Retrieve the post-webinar usage report for an ended webinar: host, topic, scheduled vs. actual times, duration, and total/unique attendee counts. Required: webinarId (path). Read-only. Requires a paid plan and report:read:admin scope. Billing-usage view, distinct from webinars_webinar's configuration view.
descriptions.report_webinar-polls: Retrieve every attendee's poll responses for an ended webinar, grouped by attendee with name, email, and per-question answers. Required: webinarId (path). Read-only. Requires a paid plan and report:read:admin scope.
descriptions.report_webinar-qa: Retrieve the Q&A transcript from an ended webinar: each asked question with the asker's name/email and the answers given. Required: webinarId (path). Read-only. Requires a paid plan, report:read:admin scope, and Q&A enabled on the webinar.
descriptions.tracking-fields_get: Retrieve a single account tracking field by id, returning its field name, recommended/required flags, and allowed values. Required: fieldId (path). Read-only. Tracking fields tag meetings/webinars for usage reporting; use tracking-fields_list to discover ids.
descriptions.tracking-fields_list: List all tracking fields configured on your Zoom account, returning each field's id, name, recommended/required flags, and visible/allowed values. Read-only. Tracking fields categorize meetings and webinars for billing and usage reports. Requires account-level (admin) auth.
descriptions.tsp_tsp: Retrieve account-level Telephony Service Provider (TSP) configuration: enabled flag, master/dial-in numbers, and call-out country settings. Read-only. Use users_tsp_user for a specific user's TSP accounts. Requires account-level (admin) auth.
descriptions.users_assistants_user: List the scheduling assistants assigned to a user, returning each assistant's id and email. Required: userId (path; accepts an email or user id). Read-only. Assistants can schedule and manage meetings on the user's behalf.
descriptions.users_assistants_user-create: Assign one or more scheduling assistants to a user. Required: userId (path); assistants[] (body) — each entry an existing user's id or email. Returns the added assistant ids. Assistants gain rights to schedule on the user's behalf; this adds to, rather than replaces, the existing list.
descriptions.users_create: Create a new user on your account. Required (body): action ('create', 'autoCreate', 'custCreate', or 'ssoCreate') and user_info with email, type (1=Basic, 2=Licensed, 3=On-prem), and optional first_name/last_name. Returns the new user's id and email. Choose 'create' to send an activation email; 'autoCreate' to provision without one. Requires account-level (admin) auth.
descriptions.users_email_user-update: Change a user's login email address. Required: userId (path); email (body, the new address). Returns HTTP 204 (no body) on success. The new address must not already belong to another Zoom user. Requires account-level (admin) auth.
descriptions.users_pac_user: List a user's Personal Audio Conference (PAC) accounts, returning each conference_id, dedicated dial-in numbers, and participant/listen-only passwords. Required: userId (path). Read-only. PAC requires the feature to be enabled on the user's plan.
descriptions.users_password_user: Set or reset a user's Zoom login password. Required: userId (path); password (body, the new value meeting account complexity rules). Returns HTTP 204 (no body) on success. State-changing; the user is signed out of active sessions. Requires account-level (admin) auth.
descriptions.users_permissions_user: Retrieve the granular feature permissions granted to a user, returned as an array of permission keys. Required: userId (path). Read-only. Use this to audit what an account user is allowed to do before changing their role or type.
descriptions.users_picture_user: Upload and set a user's profile picture. Required: userId (path); pic_file (multipart body, JPG/JPEG/PNG up to 2MB). Returns HTTP 201 on success. State-changing; replaces the existing avatar.
descriptions.users_schedulers_user: List the users who have assigned this user as their scheduling assistant (the inverse of users_assistants_user), returning each scheduler's id and email. Required: userId (path). Read-only.
descriptions.users_status_user: Activate or deactivate a user account. Required: userId (path); action (body) — 'activate' or 'deactivate'. Returns HTTP 204 (no body) on success. Deactivated users cannot sign in but retain their data; state-changing. Requires account-level (admin) auth.
descriptions.users_tsp_user: List a user's Telephony Service Provider (TSP) accounts, returning each tsp_account's id, provider, and dial-in/call-out numbers. Required: userId (path). Read-only. Use tsp_tsp for account-wide TSP config.
descriptions.users_tsp_user-tspcreate: Add a Telephony Service Provider (TSP) account to a user. Required: userId (path); tsp_bridge plus the provider's conference/dial-in credentials (body). Returns the created tsp_account id. State-changing; a user can hold up to two TSP accounts.
descriptions.webhooks_create: Create an account webhook subscription. Required (body): a public HTTPS endpoint url and an events array naming the event types to deliver. Returns the new webhook's id and status. Webhooks push event notifications (meeting.started, recording.completed, etc.) to your endpoint. Requires account-level (admin) auth.
descriptions.webhooks_delete: Delete an account webhook subscription, permanently stopping event delivery to its endpoint. Required: webhookId (path). Returns HTTP 204 (no body) on success. Destructive and irreversible — list with webhooks_webhooks to confirm the id first.
descriptions.webhooks_switch: Switch the webhook API version for the account (legacy v1 vs. current). Body: the target version. Returns HTTP 204 (no body) on success. Account-wide partial update that affects how all webhook payloads are formatted; change deliberately. Requires account-level (admin) auth.
descriptions.webhooks_update: Partially update an existing webhook subscription — change its endpoint url, the subscribed events array, or its enabled status. Required: webhookId (path); only the fields you want to change (body). Returns HTTP 204 (no body) on success. Send a partial patch, not the full object.
descriptions.webhooks_webhook: Retrieve a single webhook subscription by id, returning its endpoint url, subscribed events array, and status. Required: webhookId (path). Read-only. Use webhooks_webhooks to discover ids.
descriptions.webhooks_webhooks: List all webhook subscriptions on the account, returning each webhook's id, endpoint url, events array, and status. Read-only. Use this to audit which endpoints receive which events before creating or deleting subscriptions. Requires account-level (admin) auth.
descriptions.webinars_panelists_webinar: List the panelists invited to a webinar, returning each panelist's id, name, email, and join_url. Required: webinarId (path). Read-only. Panelists differ from attendees — they can speak and share. Use this to discover panelist ids before removing one.
descriptions.webinars_panelists_webinar-create: Add one or more panelists to a webinar. Required: webinarId (path); panelists[] (body) — each entry a name and email. Returns the added panelist ids. Each panelist receives a unique join URL granting speak/share rights. Adds to the existing panelist list.
descriptions.webinars_polls_webinar: List all polls configured on a webinar, returning each poll's id, title, status, and questions. Required: webinarId (path). Read-only. Use this to discover poll ids before webinars_polls_webinar-get or webinars_polls_webinar-update.
descriptions.webinars_polls_webinar-get: Retrieve a single poll on a webinar, including its title, status, and full question/answer-option list. Required: webinarId and pollId (both path). Read-only. Use webinars_polls_webinar first if you don't have the pollId.
descriptions.webinars_polls_webinar-update: Replace a webinar's poll definition (title and the full questions array) in place. Required: webinarId and pollId (path); title and questions (body). Returns HTTP 204 (no body) on success. Overwrites the poll, so send the complete questions list.
descriptions.webinars_status_webinar: Change a webinar's status. Required: webinarId (path); action (body) — typically 'end' to end a live webinar. Returns HTTP 204 (no body) on success. State-changing; use webinars_webinar to inspect the webinar first.
descriptions.webinars_update: Partially update a scheduled webinar's configuration — topic, start_time, duration, agenda, or settings. Required: webinarId (path); only the fields to change (body). Optional: occurrence_id to target one instance of a recurring webinar. Returns HTTP 204 (no body) on success. Send a partial patch, not the full object.
descriptions.webinars_webinar: Retrieve full details for a single webinar: topic, type, status, start_time, duration, timezone, join_url, registration_url, and settings. Required: webinarId (path). Optional: occurrence_id for a specific instance. Read-only. Use when you already have a webinar id and need its current configuration.
